message:
chore(issues): record ColdTierRetriever trait progress on ft-1memj.19

Update ft-1memj.19 with ColdTierRetriever trait implementation comment
documenting NullColdRetriever impl and cold_line/cold_page access methods
completing the interface layer for three-tier scrollback access model.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-1memj.16","title":"[gui][bundle] Update FrankenTerm.app to use custom GUI binary","description":"# Update FrankenTerm.app to use custom GUI binary

## What
Update scripts/create-macos-bundle.sh to build and bundle frankenterm-gui from source instead of copying stock wezterm-gui from /Applications/WezTerm.app.

## Current State
The bundle script copies:
- /Applications/WezTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/wezterm-gui (135 MB stock binary)
- /Applications/WezTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/wezterm (67 MB CLI)
- /Applications/WezTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/wezterm-mux-server (62 MB)

## Target State
The bundle script builds:
- crates/frankenterm-gui → Contents/MacOS/frankenterm-gui (custom binary)
- crates/frankenterm → Contents/MacOS/ft (management CLI)
- (optional) wezterm-mux-server from vendored crates

## How
1. Update create-macos-bundle.sh:
   - `cargo build --release --bin frankenterm-gui --bin ft`
   - Copy from target/release/ instead of /Applications/WezTerm.app/
   - No more --wezterm-app flag
2. Update Info.plist CFBundleExecutable from "wezterm-gui" to "frankenterm-gui"
3. Embed icon directly in the binary via build.rs (optional, macOS resource fork)
4. Update CI release workflow to build the .app bundle

## Acceptance Criteria
- `./scripts/create-macos-bundle.sh` builds everything from source
- FrankenTerm.app launches frankenterm-gui (custom binary)
- No dependency on a pre-installed WezTerm.app
- App shows "FrankenTerm" in Activity Monitor with correct icon","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-03-02T06:58:19.029316Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T22:42:25.813047Z","closed_at":"2026-03-02T22:42:25.813033Z","close_reason":"Bundle script builds from source (frankenterm-gui + ft). No WezTerm.app dependency. Info.plist points to frankenterm-gui. Default config bundled in Resources. Committed 8610c04e.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bundle","gui","macos","packaging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.16","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:58:19.029316Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.16","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:59:28.879871Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.16","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.33","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:10:02.560325Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.16","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:32:15.594926Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1691,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.16","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Review: Added dependency on .8 (config). The bundle should ship a default frankenterm.toml and the script needs to know config paths.","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:33:54Z"},{"id":1716,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.16","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"CROSS-REFERENCE: The bundle should include frankenterm-mux-server from .33, not stock wezterm-mux-server. Once .33 is implemented, update the build script to: cargo build --release --bin frankenterm-gui --bin ft --bin frankenterm-mux-server. The bundle Contents/MacOS/ should contain all three binaries.","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:09:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-1memj.17","title":"[gui][swarm] Agent-aware session management in GUI","description":"# Agent-aware session management in GUI

## What
Add agent-fleet-aware features directly in the GUI that go beyond what WezTerm provides.

## Why
WezTerm was designed for human users. FrankenTerm needs to handle 200+ AI agent sessions simultaneously. The GUI itself needs to be aware of agent workloads.

## Features
1. **Agent pane identification**: Visual indicators showing which panes are controlled by AI agents vs human-interactive
   - Color-coded tab/pane borders (green=active agent, yellow=thinking, red=stuck, gray=idle)
   - Agent name overlay in pane title bar

2. **Backpressure visualization**: Show backpressure tier directly in the pane chrome
   - Green/Yellow/Red/Black tier indicator
   - Queue depth sparkline
   - Rate limit status per pane

3. **Mass pane operations**: GUI shortcuts for agent swarm management
   - "Kill all stuck agents" (Cmd+Shift+K)
   - "Pause all agent input" (Cmd+Shift+P)
   - "Focus on error panes" (Cmd+Shift+E) — filter view to panes with detected errors

4. **Smart pane auto-layout**: When agents create many panes, auto-arrange them by:
   - Domain (group by project)
   - Status (errors first, active next, idle last)
   - Activity (most recent output on top)

5. **Resource monitoring overlay**: Show system resource usage (CPU, memory, disk) per agent pane group

## Integration Points
- Reads from ft watch daemon's health data (backpressure tiers, agent status)
- Uses the mux's CollapsePriority to auto-collapse idle agent panes
- Leverages swap layouts for dynamic rearrangement

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agent panes visually distinguished from human panes
- Backpressure tier visible in pane chrome
- Mass operations work on all agent panes simultaneously
- Auto-layout arranges 50+ panes coherently","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":360,"created_at":"2026-03-02T06:58:19.377922Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:27:04.779515Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T00:27:04.779498Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agents","gui","swarm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.17","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:08:33.759578Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.17","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:58:19.377922Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.17","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:59:29.177528Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.17","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:59:29.481099Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.17","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:39:37.296624Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.17","depends_on_id":"ft-iehgn","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-02T07:00:04.187608Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1687,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.17","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"## Test Coverage (implemented in .30)

Agent swarm stress tests:
- 50/100/200 pane scale tests (memory, CPU, frame time)
- Concurrent output throughput
- Backpressure activation under load
- Mass pane operations (kill stuck, pause all, focus errors)

Tests are tracked in ft-1memj.30 (Agent swarm stress test suite).","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:25:13Z"},{"id":1697,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.17","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Review: Consider adding macOS native notifications (UNUserNotificationCenter) for critical agent events when GUI is in background. When an agent hits a rate limit or error, the operator needs to know even if the terminal is behind other windows. This is a small addition to the agent-aware session management but high user impact.","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:40:28Z"},{"id":1707,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.17","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"GAP: The bead defines visual states (green=active, yellow=thinking, red=stuck, gray=idle) but does NOT specify the detection logic for each state. This must be defined:
- ACTIVE (green): pane produced output in last 5 seconds
- THINKING (yellow): pane has received input but produced no output for 5-30 seconds (agent is processing)
- STUCK (red): pane has produced no output for >30 seconds after receiving input, OR watchdog/circuit breaker has flagged it, OR pattern engine detected a rate limit/error
- IDLE (gray): pane has received no input AND produced no output for >60 seconds
These thresholds should be configurable in frankenterm.toml under [agent_detection]. The detection should leverage existing frankenterm-core infrastructure: watchdog (src/watchdog.rs), circuit_breaker (src/circuit_breaker.rs), and pattern engine events.","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:59:19Z"},{"id":1749,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.17","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Claimed by DustyFinch. Starting implementation of agent-aware session management GUI features.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:14:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-1memj.18","title":"[gui][swarm] Integrated TUI dashboard within GUI (not separate ft tui)","description":"# Integrated TUI dashboard within GUI (not separate ft tui)

## What
Embed the TUI dashboard (currently `ft tui`, a separate terminal app) as a built-in panel/tab within frankenterm-gui itself.

## Why
Currently the dashboard runs as a separate terminal process (`ft tui`) that the user opens in a terminal. This is circular — you need a terminal to view the terminal management dashboard. The dashboard should be a native panel within the GUI.

## How
1. Add a "Dashboard" tab type to frankenterm-gui (alongside regular terminal tabs)
2. Render the dashboard content using the GPU renderer (not via a PTY)
3. Dashboard reads from the same data sources as `ft tui`:
   - SQLite storage for events, pane history, search
   - Live pane state from mux
   - Health data from watchdog/backpressure/circuit breaker
4. Allow toggling dashboard with a keybinding (e.g., Cmd+Shift+D)
5. Dashboard should be a side panel or overlay, not replacing the terminal view

## Design Inspiration
- Zellij's built-in status bar and session manager
- tmux's built-in status line and chooser
- iTerm2's "Toolbelt" side panel

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dashboard accessible via keybinding without leaving the GUI
- Shows: pane list, health, events, search — same as ft tui
- Rendered natively (not via a PTY running ft tui)
- Can interact with dashboard while terminals remain visible","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-02T06:58:19.725007Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:33:20.650346Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T00:33:20.650326Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","gui","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.18","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:58:19.725007Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.18","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:59:29.782517Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1710,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.18","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"UX REQUIREMENT: The embedded dashboard's search results must be clickable/navigable — selecting a search result should focus the corresponding pane and scroll to the matched content. This is the 'search → jump to pane → see context' workflow that makes FrankenTerm dramatically better than running ft search in a separate terminal. Implementation: search results include pane_id and approximate line number; clicking a result sends a FocusPane(pane_id) + ScrollToLine(line) command to the mux.","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:00:20Z"},{"id":1756,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.18","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Claimed by DustyFinch. Building on ft-1memj.17 agent state types for dashboard integration.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:30:05Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-1memj.19","title":"[gui][perf] Memory-efficient scrollback for 200+ pane agent swarms","description":"# Memory-efficient scrollback for 200+ pane agent swarms

## What
Implement tiered scrollback storage that prevents OOM when running 200+ agent panes with large output volumes.

## Problem
WezTerm stores all scrollback in RAM. With default scrollback (3500 lines), 200 panes = 200 * 3500 * ~200 bytes/line = ~140 MB just for scrollback. Agent sessions with CI output can produce 100k+ lines per pane, pushing memory to 4+ GB for scrollback alone.

## Architecture
Three-tier scrollback:
1. **Hot tier** (RAM): Last N lines (configurable, default 1000). Instant access for rendering.
2. **Warm tier** (mmap): Compressed scrollback pages on disk, memory-mapped for fast random access. Uses the vendored `DirtyRect` tracking to know which pages need writing.
3. **Cold tier** (SQLite): Historical scrollback beyond the warm tier. Searchable via FTS. Already partially implemented in ft watch's capture pipeline.

## Integration with Existing Work
- ft-8vla (mmap-backed scrollback storage) is an existing P4 bead — this supersedes it with a more comprehensive design
- The recording pipeline (ft watch) already captures scrollback to SQLite — extend this for the warm→cold tier transition
- The existing BackpressureManager can signal when to flush warm→cold (Red/Black tier = aggressive eviction)

## How
1. Implement `TieredScrollback` in vendored term crate
2. Hot→warm transition: when hot buffer exceeds N lines, zstd-compress oldest pages and mmap to disk
3. Warm→cold transition: when warm tier exceeds M MB, flush oldest pages to SQLite via ft watch
4. Access pattern: scrolling up seamlessly decompresses warm pages on demand
5. Per-pane eviction: idle panes evict more aggressively than active panes
6. Config:
   ```toml
   [scrollback]
   hot_lines = 1000
   warm_max_mb = 50
   cold_storage = "sqlite"
   compression = "zstd"
   ```

## Acceptance Criteria
- 200 panes with 100k lines each: memory usage < 1 GB (vs ~4+ GB in stock WezTerm)
- Scroll-up latency < 50ms for warm tier pages
- No visible stuttering when crossing tier boundaries
- Backpressure integration: Red/Black tier triggers aggressive eviction","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":360,"created_at":"2026-03-02T06:58:20.040031Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-23T06:47:18.154777Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gui","memory","performance","swarm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:58:20.040031Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:59:30.082356Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:32:15.935139Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","depends_on_id":"ft-8vla","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-02T07:00:03.890327Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","depends_on_id":"ft-iehgn","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:08:32.933363Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1692,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Review: Added dependency on .7 (resize reflow). Tiered scrollback warm page decompression interacts with resize reflow - scorecard determines if decompressed pages should be reflowed or kept as-is.","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:33:55Z"},{"id":1704,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"CODEBASE VERIFICATION: The vendored term crate already has ColdScrollbackReflowWorker (screen.rs:401-460) that handles async scrollback reflow with: intent tracking (SequenceNo), backlog depth caps (COLD_SCROLLBACK_BACKLOG_DEPTH_CAP), throughput telemetry, batch completion, and cancellation for stale resize intents. The tiered scrollback implementation should integrate with this existing worker rather than reimplementing from scratch. The cold reflow worker already handles the pattern of 'process scrollback in batches without blocking the main render path' which is exactly what the warm-to-cold tier transition needs.","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:46:11Z"},{"id":1740,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","author":"DustyLynx","text":"Implemented first tiered-scrollback slice: added ScrollbackTierConfig surface in frankenterm/term, plumbed new config keys (scrollback_tiered_enabled, scrollback_hot_lines, scrollback_warm_max_mb) through frankenterm/config, and updated Screen hot-tier capacity enforcement plus warm/cold spill accounting telemetry. Added parsing/bridge tests in config crates and tier behavior tests in term screen tests. Validation attempts used rch exec with isolated CARGO_TARGET_DIRs but remote workers failed open and local cargo checks timed out repeatedly under heavy workspace contention; no clean cargo check artifact yet.","created_at":"2026-03-02T23:51:24Z"},{"id":1741,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","author":"DustyLynx","text":"Second slice landed in frankenterm/term/src/screen.rs: ScrollbackTieringState::record_spill now returns explicit cold overflow outcome; Screen::record_scrollback_spill now threads seqno and routes warm->cold overflow events through existing ColdScrollbackReflowWorker telemetry (begin_intent/complete_cold_batch/finish_intent) instead of standalone counters only. Added test tiered_scrollback_cold_spill_updates_worker_metrics to assert cold overflow increments worker completion/throughput telemetry and leaves backlog at zero. Formatting: rustfmt --edition 2024 frankenterm/term/src/screen.rs. Validation attempts (per policy via rch): rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-dustylynx-ft1memj19 cargo test -p frankenterm-term tiered_scrollback -- --nocapture; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-dustylynx-ft1memj19 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 cargo test -p frankenterm-term tiered_scrollback_cold_spill_updates_worker_metrics -- --nocapture. Both failed-open locally because all rch workers were unreachable (rch workers probe --all = all 8 connection failed), and local compile was terminated by external SIGTERM during build scripts, so no clean cargo test artifact yet.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:02:03Z"},{"id":1743,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","author":"DustyLynx","text":"Follow-up in same slice: corrected tiered_scrollback_enforces_hot_line_budget test assertion to account for visible rows (scrollback_rows() currently returns total in-memory rows). Assertion now checks <= physical_rows + hot_lines semantics.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:03:10Z"},{"id":1765,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","author":"DustyLynx","text":"Fresh-eyes audit complete: fixed Config::load_with_overrides fallback so override values apply even when no frankenterm.toml is present (non-lua and lua-disabled paths) via defaults_with_overrides(). Regression validated with rch-offloaded targeted tests: frankenterm-term zero_scrollback_preserves_no_history, frankenterm-term tiered_scrollback, config term_config_maps_resize_wrap_controls_from_config_handle, config wasm_overrides_applied, config basic_toml_fields_parse.","created_at":"2026-03-03T04:37:19Z"},{"id":2336,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","author":"SilentTower","text":"SilentTower progress 2026-03-13: claimed bounded pane render protocol seam for tiered scrollback diagnostics. Landed mux-side PaneTieredScrollbackStatus type, Pane::get_tiered_scrollback_status default hook, local/termwiz pane implementations, render PDU payload plumbing, sessionhandler diff tracking, client-side cache, and compile-only response scaffolding updates. Validation: cargo fmt --check clean after formatting. rch-selected cargo check synced remotely then failed open to local execution when the worker became unreachable; final build failure was unrelated in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs (missing MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS import in test scope), outside this slice and inside another agent's claimed file.","created_at":"2026-03-13T23:01:28Z"},{"id":2480,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","author":"jemanuel","text":"ChartreusePanther: Added 4 FleetScrollbackCoordinator integration tests at 200-pane scale: emergency eviction, critical partial eviction, normal no-eviction, and memory-under-1GB acceptance criterion. Commit fa977dbd. Total: 18 integration tests passing.","created_at":"2026-03-23T06:40:02Z"},{"id":2481,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","author":"jemanuel","text":"ChartreusePanther: Added 3 coordinator stress tests in e2e_swarm_stress_core.rs: emergency at 200 panes, multi-tick escalation, and recovery after pressure subsides. Commit 9946eaa7. Total deliverables for this session: FleetScrollbackCoordinator module (18 tests), 6 proptest cases, 4 integration tests at 200-pane scale, 3 stress tests.","created_at":"2026-03-23T06:47:18Z"},{"id":2478,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","author":"jemanuel","text":"ChartreusePanther: Implemented FleetScrollbackCoordinator bridging FleetMemoryController + FleetScrollbackOrchestrator + TieredScrollback. 18 unit tests + 6 proptests, all passing. Commit 21ef30ad.","created_at":"2026-03-23T06:37:23Z"}]}
+{"id":"ft-1memj.19","title":"[gui][perf] Memory-efficient scrollback for 200+ pane agent swarms","description":"# Memory-efficient scrollback for 200+ pane agent swarms

## What
Implement tiered scrollback storage that prevents OOM when running 200+ agent panes with large output volumes.

## Problem
WezTerm stores all scrollback in RAM. With default scrollback (3500 lines), 200 panes = 200 * 3500 * ~200 bytes/line = ~140 MB just for scrollback. Agent sessions with CI output can produce 100k+ lines per pane, pushing memory to 4+ GB for scrollback alone.

## Architecture
Three-tier scrollback:
1. **Hot tier** (RAM): Last N lines (configurable, default 1000). Instant access for rendering.
2. **Warm tier** (mmap): Compressed scrollback pages on disk, memory-mapped for fast random access. Uses the vendored `DirtyRect` tracking to know which pages need writing.
3. **Cold tier** (SQLite): Historical scrollback beyond the warm tier. Searchable via FTS. Already partially implemented in ft watch's capture pipeline.

## Integration with Existing Work
- ft-8vla (mmap-backed scrollback storage) is an existing P4 bead — this supersedes it with a more comprehensive design
- The recording pipeline (ft watch) already captures scrollback to SQLite — extend this for the warm→cold tier transition
- The existing BackpressureManager can signal when to flush warm→cold (Red/Black tier = aggressive eviction)

## How
1. Implement `TieredScrollback` in vendored term crate
2. Hot→warm transition: when hot buffer exceeds N lines, zstd-compress oldest pages and mmap to disk
3. Warm→cold transition: when warm tier exceeds M MB, flush oldest pages to SQLite via ft watch
4. Access pattern: scrolling up seamlessly decompresses warm pages on demand
5. Per-pane eviction: idle panes evict more aggressively than active panes
6. Config:
   ```toml
   [scrollback]
   hot_lines = 1000
   warm_max_mb = 50
   cold_storage = "sqlite"
   compression = "zstd"
   ```

## Acceptance Criteria
- 200 panes with 100k lines each: memory usage < 1 GB (vs ~4+ GB in stock WezTerm)
- Scroll-up latency < 50ms for warm tier pages
- No visible stuttering when crossing tier boundaries
- Backpressure integration: Red/Black tier triggers aggressive eviction","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":360,"created_at":"2026-03-02T06:58:20.040031Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-23T07:59:01.645303Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gui","memory","performance","swarm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:58:20.040031Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:59:30.082356Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:32:15.935139Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","depends_on_id":"ft-8vla","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-02T07:00:03.890327Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","depends_on_id":"ft-iehgn","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:08:32.933363Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1692,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Review: Added dependency on .7 (resize reflow). The tiered scrollback implementation should integrate with this existing worker rather than reimplementing from scratch. The cold reflow worker already handles the pattern of 'process scrollback in batches without blocking the main render path' which is exactly what the warm-to-cold tier transition needs.","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:46:11Z"},{"id":1740,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.19","author":"DustyLynx","text":"Implemented first tiered-scrollback slice: added ScrollbackTierConfig surface in frankenterm/term, plumbed new config keys (scrollback_tiered_enabled, scrollback_hot_lines, scrollback_warm_max_mb) through frankenterm/config, and updated Screen hot-tier capacity enforcement plus warm/cold spill accounting telemetry. Added parsing/bridge tests in config crates and tier behavior tests in term screen tests.  {"id":"ft-1memj.2","title":"[gui][bootstrap] Vendor window/font/client crates from legacy_wezterm","description":"# Vendor window/font/client crates from legacy_wezterm

## What
Copy and adapt the remaining WezTerm GUI-layer crates into the `frankenterm/` vendored directory so that `frankenterm-gui` can be built entirely from vendored sources.

## Why
The vendored `frankenterm/mux`, `frankenterm/term`, etc. are the backend. But `wezterm-gui` also needs:
- `window/` — platform windowing (Cocoa on macOS, X11/Wayland on Linux), OpenGL context, input handling
- `wezterm-font/` — font loading, shaping (harfbuzz), rasterization, fallback chains
- `wezterm-client/` — mux client for connecting to mux servers, domain management
- `wezterm-gui-subcommands/` — CLI parsing for GUI-specific flags (--class, --position, etc.)
- `frecency/`, `lfucache/` — utility crates used by font cache

These are currently only in `legacy_wezterm/` which is a frozen git snapshot, not a workspace member.

## How
1. Copy each crate into `frankenterm/`:
   - `frankenterm/window/` ← `legacy_wezterm/window/`
   - `frankenterm/font/` ← `legacy_wezterm/wezterm-font/`
   - `frankenterm/client/` ← `legacy_wezterm/wezterm-client/`
   - `frankenterm/gui-subcommands/` ← `legacy_wezterm/wezterm-gui-subcommands/`
   - `frankenterm/frecency/` ← `legacy_wezterm/frecency/`
   - `frankenterm/lfucache/` ← `legacy_wezterm/lfucache/`

2. Update their Cargo.tomls to reference sibling vendored crates (not upstream paths)

3. Add all to workspace `members`

4. Fix any compilation issues from version drift between the mux/term modifications and the GUI layer

## Risks
- The `window` crate has platform-specific code (Cocoa FFI on macOS, X11 on Linux) — may need #[cfg] adjustments
- Font crate depends on harfbuzz-sys, freetype-sys — heavy native deps
- Version skew between modified mux/term and unmodified window/font may cause trait mismatches

## Acceptance Criteria
- All vendored crates compile as workspace members
- No references to `legacy_wezterm/` paths remain in any Cargo.toml
- `cargo check -p frankenterm-gui` resolves all deps from vendored crates","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonDune","estimated_minutes":480,"created_at":"2026-03-02T06:54:43.062471Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T19:31:38.220335Z","closed_at":"2026-03-02T19:31:38.220258Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bootstrap","gui","vendoring"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.2","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:54:43.062471Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.2","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:59:23.787592Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-1memj.20","title":"[gui][mux] Built-in session manager (Zellij-style)","description":"# Built-in session manager (Zellij-style)

## What
Add a native session manager to frankenterm-gui, inspired by Zellij's session management.

## Why
Zellij's killer feature is persistent named sessions with instant attach/detach. For agent swarms, this means:
- Named sessions per project ("frankenterm-dev", "api-server", "agent-fleet-alpha")
- Instant session switching without losing state
- Session templates: pre-configured layouts for common workflows
- Session sharing: multiple users/agents can attach to the same session

## Features
1. **Session list overlay**: Keybinding (e.g., Cmd+S) shows all sessions with:
   - Name, creation time, last activity
   - Pane count and layout preview (minimap)
   - Active/detached status

2. **Named sessions**: `frankenterm-gui --session agent-fleet` creates or attaches to named session

3. **Session templates**: TOML-defined session configurations:
   ```toml
   [sessions.agent-fleet]
   layout = "tall"
   panes = [
     { command = "ft watch --foreground", name = "watcher" },
     { command = "ft tui", name = "dashboard" },
     { split = "horizontal", command = "zsh", name = "shell" },
   ]
   ```

4. **Session persistence**: Sessions survive GUI crashes/restarts
   - Layout state saved to disk on every change
   - Restore on reattach (leverages existing SnapshotEngine)

5. **Session sharing**: Multiple frankenterm-gui instances can connect to the same mux server session (already supported by WezTerm's mux protocol, but not exposed in a user-friendly way)

## Integration
- Uses the vendored mux's existing domain/workspace infrastructure
- Layout restore uses SnapshotEngine (already captures TopologySnapshot to SQLite)
- Session templates use the same layout types as swap layouts

## Acceptance Criteria
- Named sessions work: create, attach, detach, reattach
- Session list overlay shows all sessions
- Session templates create pre-configured layouts
- Sessions survive GUI restart","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonTower","estimated_minutes":360,"created_at":"2026-03-02T06:59:00.403987Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-22T04:03:12.015587Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gui","mux","sessions","zellij"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.20","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:59:00.403987Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.20","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T06:59:30.381484Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.20","depends_on_id":"ft-1memj.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:32:16.291074Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1memj.20","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.6","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:08:32.532762Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1693,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.20","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Review: Added dependency on .8 (config). Session templates are TOML-defined, so the config system must parse them.","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:33:55Z"},{"id":1709,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.20","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"NOTE: The session manager should support 'frankenterm-gui --connect host:port' for attaching to a remote frankenterm-mux-server. This is WezTerm's multiplexer domain pattern — the client crate (vendored in .2) already supports this. The session list overlay should show both local and remote sessions, with remote sessions showing the connection status (connected/reconnecting/disconnected).","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:59:55Z"},{"id":1711,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.20","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"CRASH RECOVERY DETAIL: When the GUI restarts after a crash, the session restore must handle these cases:
1. PTY processes still alive (normal crash): reconnect to existing PTYs via mux server (if mux survived) or via PID + /dev/pts mapping
2. PTY processes dead (hard crash or reboot): restore layout from SnapshotEngine, show placeholder text in each pane ('Session restored - original process exited')
3. Partial restore: some PTYs alive, some dead — restore what's possible, mark dead panes
4. The mux server (.33 headless binary) should be the default for agent swarms — it survives GUI crashes since it's a separate process. GUI reconnects to it on restart.
This makes the mux-server separation (.33) architecturally important for reliability, not just for headless use.","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:01:53Z"},{"id":1713,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.20","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"PRIORITY CHANGE P2->P1: Session persistence is NOT optional for agent swarms. Without named sessions + crash recovery, closing the GUI kills all agent sessions permanently. This makes the session manager a reliability feature, not a convenience feature. Also, the headless mux server (.33) is architecturally linked — the mux server IS the persistent session that survives GUI crashes.","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:02:47Z"},{"id":1750,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.20","author":"FoggyRobin","text":"Implemented first slice for named sessions: added `--session` as a visible alias for `--workspace` on GUI Start and Connect commands in frankenterm/gui-subcommands/src/lib.rs, and added parser tests `start_command_accepts_session_alias` + `connect_command_accepts_session_alias`. Updated docs/frankenterm-gui-user-guide.md examples to include `--session`. Validation attempts were run via rch (`rch exec -- ... cargo test -p frankenterm-gui-subcommands ...`) but remote worker repeatedly failed-over to local and local cargo remained blocked in shared compile contention; no deterministic test pass/fail artifact yet. Keeping bead in_progress and continuing with conflict-free slices.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:19:38Z"},{"id":1752,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.20","author":"FoggyRobin","text":"Second incremental slice landed: set default hotkey for launcher/session surface in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/commands.rs (`ShowLauncher` now binds to SUPER+s, which also synthesizes CTRL+SHIFT fallback on platforms where SUPER is reserved). Updated docs/frankenterm-gui-user-guide.md to call out Cmd+S session-manager entry point. This keeps implementation conflict-free while termwindow/mod.rs is actively modified by another agent.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:20:56Z"},{"id":1836,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.20","author":"CrimsonTower","text":"Progress slice (session-manager launcher metadata):\n- Updated crates/frankenterm-gui/src/overlay/launcher.rs to materialize workspace/session entries with active-state + window/pane counts, sort active session first, and present session-focused labels in the launcher list.\n- Updated crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/mod.rs so ShowLauncher presents title 'Session Manager' with session-oriented help text.\n- Updated docs/frankenterm-gui-user-guide.md to document that Cmd+S session rows now include active marker and window/pane counts.\n\nValidation (all via rch exec -- ... as required):\n1) cargo check -p frankenterm-gui --all-targets -> blocked by pre-existing shapecache test-surface errors (missing k9/benchmarking deps), unrelated to this slice.\n2) cargo check -p frankenterm-gui --bin frankenterm-gui -> blocked by pre-existing Cmd+S opens the session-manager surface, so Enter defaults to current session context.","created_at":"2026-03-04T01:38:24Z"},{"id":1838,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.20","author":"CrimsonTower","text":"Correction: the follow-up preselection is implemented by setting active_idx to the current session entry while building workspace/session rows.","created_at":"2026-03-04T01:38:32Z"},{"id":2453,"issue_id":"ft-1memj.20","author":"MossyMoose","text":"MossyMoose bounded support slice (2026-03-22): hardened the session-manager launcher in `crates/frankenterm-gui/src/overlay/launcher.rs` without broadening the active session-manager feature scope. Production changes: (1) guard launcher navigation when filtering yields zero matches so `move_down()` cannot underflow on an empty filtered list; (2) compute quickselect labels only for the currently visible viewport rows so scrolled launchers do not expose hidden labels that map past the end of the visible list; and (3) clamp mouse row -> entry selection to the filtered entry bounds near the bottom of a scrolled list. Added focused unit coverage for the new pure helpers backing those cases. Lightweight hygiene: `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-gui/src/overlay/launcher.rs` PASS; `git diff --check -- crates/frankenterm-gui/src/overlay/launcher.rs` PASS. Heavy validation is running compliantly via `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-gui --bin frankenterm-gui overlay::launcher::tests -- --nocapture` on worker `vmi1152480`; unlike earlier stale runs, this one completed remote sync/exec and is still active in RCH queue/status rather than fail-opening locally. Agent Mail intro/contact attempts remain intermittently blocked by transient DB errors, so this bead comment is the durable coordination record for the slice.","created_at":"2026-03-22T04:03:12Z"}]}
